brief_document.title: aion_client (Gleam, Hex): connect, operations, encoder/decoder payloads, event stream, error type
blocked_by: AW: aion-server API + aion-proto wire contract must be defined
brief_document.purpose: Build the Gleam caller SDK so Gleam services — including those authoring workflows with aion_flow — can drive workflows from outside with full static typing. It implements the AL-001 contract in Gleam idioms: connect plus the operations, encoder/decoder-based payloads with a raw escape hatch, an event stream that resumes across disconnects, and an error custom type. gleam check, format, and test clean.
brief_document.task: Create the aion_client Gleam package (Hex name aion_client). Implement connect plus start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe with start returning a workflow handle value, gleam/json encoder + gleam/dynamic decoder typed payloads with a raw-Payload escape hatch, a subscribe event stream with sequence-based resumption, and an error custom type over the shared taxonomy. Behaviour must match the AL-001 contract; the conformance harness is AL-007, but the package must be drivable by it. Use the established beamr/Gleam HTTP + WebSocket capabilities for transport.

requirements.title: Package scaffold, connect, and operations
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the aion_client package exposing connect plus start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe, with start returning a handle value usable in per-workflow operations. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require reconnecting per operation.
acceptance: gleam/aion_client/gleam.toml declares name aion_client, the Hex metadata, and the gleam_stdlib / gleam_json / HTTP-client dependencies.
aion_client.gleam exposes connect(config) returning a Client value, and start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe functions taking the Client.
start returns a WorkflowHandle value carrying workflow_id + run_id; handle.gleam offers signal/query/cancel/describe/subscribe taking that handle.
Operations target the latest run by default and a specific run_id when a Some(run_id) is supplied, per the contract.
Connection and credential failures map to the Unavailable and Unauthenticated error variants (R2).

requirements.title: Error custom type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an error custom type mapping the shared taxonomy and return Result(_, Error) from every operation. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT collapse distinct conditions into one variant.
acceptance: error.gleam defines a public Error type with variants NotFound, AlreadyExists, QueryFailed, QueryTimeout, Cancelled, Unavailable, Unauthenticated, NamespaceDenied(detail: String), InvalidArgument, and Server(detail: String).
A mapping function converts a server/transport failure into the correct variant; a test asserts the NotFound, AlreadyExists, QueryTimeout, and Unavailable mappings.
Every operation returns Result(value, Error); none returns a bare value that hides a failure.
The variant set matches the AL-001 taxonomy exactly (no extra, no missing).

requirements.title: Encoder/decoder typed payloads with raw escape hatch
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL take a gleam/json encoder for input and a gleam/dynamic decoder for the result, staying statically typed, and SHALL keep a raw-Payload escape hatch on every payload-bearing operation. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT remove the raw path.
acceptance: payload.gleam provides encode(value, encoder) -> Payload (JSON content-type) and decode(payload, decoder) -> Result(value, Error).
start/signal accept an input value plus its encoder; query accepts an args encoder and a result decoder and returns the decoded reply.
A raw variant of each payload-bearing operation accepts a Payload (bytes + content-type) directly.
A decode failure returns Error InvalidArgument (or a documented variant), not a panic or a swallowed result.
A test round-trips a value through encode then decode and asserts equality.

requirements.title: subscribe event stream with resumption
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement subscribe returning an event stream. WHILE open, IF the connection drops transiently, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reconnect and resume from the last delivered sequence number with no gaps or duplicates. IF the subscription fails terminally, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL surface the error through the stream rather than ending silently.
acceptance: stream.gleam exposes subscribe returning a stream/subject of decoded events over the WebSocket transport.
The stream tracks the last delivered sequence number and resumes from sequence+1 after a transient disconnect.
A test with a stub transport that drops mid-stream asserts each event is delivered exactly once across the reconnect.
A terminal failure is delivered as an error value through the stream channel, distinguishable from a normal end-of-stream.
gleam check passes for the stream module.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the wire protocol or server endpoints (AW).
SHALL NOT depend on aion_flow (the authoring SDK) — this is the caller client, a distinct package.
SHALL NOT implement the cross-language conformance harness — that is AL-007.
verification: The public aion_client module re-exports the seven operations and the handle/error types.
No operation panics on a failure path; every failure flows through Result or the stream's error channel.

checklist.text: aion_client connects to an aion-server endpoint with auth + TLS config and exposes connect, start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe; start returns a workflow handle value usable in per-workflow operations.
Payload helpers take a gleam/json encoder for input and a gleam/dynamic decoder for the result, staying statically typed, with a raw-Payload escape hatch on every payload-bearing operation.
subscribe returns an event stream that resumes from the last delivered sequence number across transient disconnects and surfaces terminal failure through the stream rather than ending silently.
Errors are a Gleam custom type mapping the shared taxonomy; gleam check, gleam format --check, and gleam test pass.

stories.text: As an application developer, I want to add a client dependency, point it at a server URL, and start a workflow by type name with a typed input so that I can trigger durable work from my service without learning the engine internals.
As an application developer, I want start to return a handle that lets me signal, query, cancel, and describe the workflow I just started so that I do not have to plumb IDs through my own code.
As an application developer, I want a typed result back from a query and a typed input accepted by a signal so that my compiler catches payload-shape mistakes before runtime.

As a dashboard developer, I want to subscribe to a workflow's live event stream and have the client resume transparently across transient disconnects so that I render a gap-free, duplicate-free timeline without writing reconnection logic.

constraints.text: Clients consume the aion-server API and aion-proto wire types; they SHALL NOT define their own wire formats, endpoints, or fields the server does not serve. The protocol is owned by AW.

Clients SHALL NOT depend on engine internals (aion, aion-store, beamr). The Rust client may depend on aion-core (shared domain types) and aion-proto; the embedded constructor may depend on aion only behind a feature flag, never by default.
All four SDKs expose the same seven operations with identical observable behaviour, verified by the shared conformance suite.
Every SDK exposes both a typed payload surface (per language) and a raw-Payload escape hatch; neither is removable.
Every SDK maps server failures to a documented, branchable error taxonomy. No swallowed errors, no single catch-all collapsing distinct failures.

subscribe resumes transparently across transient disconnects using the per-event sequence number, delivering a gap-free duplicate-free stream; terminal failures surface through the stream, not as a silent end.
start honours a caller-supplied idempotency key: a retried identical start returns the original handle; a conflicting reuse raises AlreadyExists.

Per-ecosystem toolchain gates pass: Rust (clippy -D warnings, fmt --check, no allow/expect/ignore), Python (ruff + mypy --strict + pytest), TypeScript (tsc strict + eslint + tests), Gleam (gleam format --check + gleam check + gleam test). No-partial-implementation and no-silent-failure standards hold across all four.
Each package is publishable to its registry (crates.io, PyPI, npm, Hex) with a README, a type-checked public surface, and a runnable example covering all seven operations.

resolved_context.intention: Ship the caller-side SDKs for the workflow-caller role: aion-client (Rust, crates.io), aion-client-python (PyPI), aion-client-typescript (npm), and aion_client (Gleam, Hex). Each connects to an aion-server deployment and exposes the same seven operations — connect, start, signal, query, cancel, list, describe, subscribe — in the idioms of its language. A developer who has never read the engine internals should add a dependency, point at a server URL, and start a workflow with full type-checking in their language within minutes; a frontend developer using the TypeScript client should never see beamr, Gleam, or Rust. The four SDKs must behave identically where behaviour is observable, proven by a shared language-neutral behavioural contract and a conformance suite that exercises every SDK against a real server.
